manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: waitress, manger, wildlife, bra, liter, ravine, exam, deficit, mumps.
Your opponent's team words are: impostor, groundhog, ignorance, info, hypocrite, graveyard, psychopath, income.
Distractor words are: broker, raincoat, allergy, tact, armpit, nighttime, vinegar.
The kill words are: peninsula.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue relating to some of our team's words. Must not be any of the listed words. Must not contain parts of them. Provide clue and list of target words we are hinting at.

Our team words: waitress, manger, wildlife, bra, liter (maybe "liter"), ravine, exam, deficit, mumps.

Goal: choose clue that ties multiple. But must be safe: not overlap with opponent or distractor or kill.

Potential clue: "hospital" relates to mumps (disease), exam (medical exam), deficit? maybe not. Could also relate to wildlife? not.

Clue "nurse" relates to mumps (patient), exam (nurse gives), deficit? maybe not; also bra? Not.

Clue "cattle" relates to manger (where animals eat), wildlife (animals), maybe not.

Clue "measurement" relates to liter, deficit? maybe not.

Clue "school" relates to exam, deficit (budget deficit?), maybe not.

Clue "soup" relates to manger? no.

Clue "costume" maybe bra, wildlife? not.

Clue "mountain" relates to ravine, wildlife (mountain wildlife), maybe not.

Clue "nutrition" maybe deficit, wildlife, mumps? not.

We have to avoid clue being any listed word or part: can't contain "exam", "mumps", etc. Also can't contain "ground", "info", etc.

Potential clue "zoo" relates to wildlife, maybe bra (zoo bra? no). Also "manger" relates to feeding animals, not zoo.

Clue "feeding" could connect to waitress (serves), manager? Waiter not manager. But feeding relates to wildlife. Also "manger" is feeding trough. Might also relate to deficit (lack of feeding). Could be ambiguous.

Better pick clue that clearly maps to several: "animal" relates to wildlife, manger (animal feed), maybe also mumps (affects mammals). Probably too vague.

We need to give list of target words we intend. Could be 2-3 words. Safer.

Maybe clue "herd" connects to wildlife (herd animals) and manger (feed trough). Also maybe "deficit" no.

But "herd" not in list.

Clue "dairy" connects to waitress? maybe not. "Manger" (milk feeding). "Bra" no.

Clue "school" connects to exam, deficit (school deficit?), maybe "mumps" (outbreak in schools). Could be plausible.

We need to ensure opponent's words not associated: "info", "groundhog", etc. "school" not.

Distractors: "raincoat", "allergy", "tact", etc. Not overlapping.

Kill word: peninsula not relevant.

Maybe give clue "health" relates to mumps, deficit (health deficit?), exam (health exam). Also wildlife? not.

But maybe better to target 3 words: exam, mumps, deficit (all health/medical). Clue "medical" might hint exam (medical exam), mumps (viral disease), deficit (medical deficiency). That seems decent.

Check that "medical" is not any listed word. It doesn't contain parts? It contains "medic", not in list.

But opponent words "hypocrite", "psychopath" are mental health but not directly.

Distractor "allergy" is medical too; but clue "medical" could also mistakenly lead to "allergy". But that's a distractor, which would end turn if guessed incorrectly; but still risk.

Maybe choose clue "test" relates to exam, deficit (test deficit?), maybe not.

Clue "virus" relates to mumps (viral), wildlife? not.

Clue "batteries"? no.

Maybe we target two high-value: "waitress" and "bra". Clue "uniform" relates to waitress uniform, bra is under clothing uniform? Not great.

Clue "service" relates to waitress, maybe also deficit? Not.

Clue "feeding" seems good for "manger" and "wildlife". Also could hint "ravine"? animals feed there. Let's propose clue "feeding".

Check: does any opponent word contain part of "feeding"? Opponent list: "impostor", "groundhog", "ignorance", "info", "hypocrite", "graveyard", "psychopath", "income". No. Distractors: "broker", "raincoat", "allergy", "tact", "armpit", "nighttime", "vinegar". No. Kill: peninsula no.

Thus safe.

Targets: manger, wildlife. Also maybe "ravine" (feeding area?). Might be too many.

We'll give clue "feeding" targeting "manger, wildlife
